name: Cancel GTT Order
summary: Cancels an existing GTT (Good Till Triggered) alert/order via a POST call. The request supplies the user id (uid) and the alert id (al_id) of the GTT order to cancel; a successful response echoes the al_id with a request_time and a delete-success status.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: GTT order success or failure indication. On success the sample shows the value "Oi delete success"; on failure "Not_Ok".

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: al_id
response_fields.description: Alert Id (echoed back; the success sample JSON key is "Al_id").

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input  2) Session Expired

notes:
- Real base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI ; the curl sample uses a placeholder 'https://BaseURL/CancelGTTOrder'.
- The POST body is form-style: jData={...JSON...} and jKey=... joined with '&' (not a pure JSON body), despite the Content-Type: application/json header in the curl sample.
- Neither uid nor al_id is marked mandatory in the doc (no trailing '*'); only the jData and jKey request parameters are starred. In practice al_id identifies which GTT order to cancel, so it is effectively required.
- Success response is a JSON array; failure response is a JSON object.
- Response key casing differs from the field table: the success sample uses "Al_id" (capital A) while the doc field list calls it al_id.
- emsg appears only on error and conveys either 1) Invalid Input or 2) Session Expired.
issues: The Possible value column for both jData json fields (uid, al_id) and for all response fields is blank in the source table - confirmed empty via the page-33 image, not a misalignment. No possible-value enums exist for this section. The response field al_id is rendered as "Al_id" (capital A) in the success sample JSON, a casing inconsistency in the source doc.
